_note: PLAN design/slot_handler/PLAN.md Stage 5: when the instance script declares `interface $$Slots` / `type $$Slots`, the render-fn return uses that as the slots surface — synthesised slot-defs are ignored. Mirrors upstream `createRenderFunction.ts:125-133` `uses$$SlotsInterface` behavior. Pre-Stage-5 native always emitted the synthesised slot-def from `<slot label="hi">` (which would type `label` as the literal `"hi"` string), overriding the user's declared `interface $$Slots { default: { label: string } }`. Post-Stage-5 the declaration wins: `label` types as `string`, and the consumer's `takesNumber(label)` call (declared `(n: number)`) fires TS2345 against the declared `string` type. New helper `has_strict_slots_ast` walks parsed_instance only — module-script `$$Slots` doesn't count, mirroring the same rule applied for `$$Events`.
